zh.Upton Pyne, Devon, England: 英國戴文厄普頓 品
zh.lifeSummary: 當Herbert de Pyn於1140年出生在英國戴文厄普頓 品時，他的父親Simon de Pyn20歲，母親Mrs Simon de Pyn19歲。他和Mrs Herbert de Pyn至少有1個兒子。他去世於1219年在英國英格蘭享年79歲。

zh-hans.Upton Pyne, Devon, England: 英国戴文厄普頓 品

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Pyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Yong, Tao, Hong, Hui, Ming, Dong, Jing, Ning, Rong, Chen.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 潘, meaning ‘rice water’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Pan (潘), a fief (located in present-day northwestern Shangqiu in Henan province) that was granted to Ji Sun, son of Bi Gong Gao (Duke of Bi, the 15th son of King Wen of Zhou, 1152–1056  BC ). (ii) borne by descendants of Pan Chong (潘崇), a noble in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) adopted as a surname by the Po Duo Luo (破多罗) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) adopted as a surname by Pan Shao (潘紹), who changed his original surname Zhao to Pan during the Yuan dynasty (1206–1368  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 盤, denoting ‘washbasin’ or ‘plate’ in ancient Chinese: said to be borne by descendants of Pan Hu (盤瓠), an ancient legendary figure. They were originally scattered around the present-day southern Sichuan province and later migrated to Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region and Guangdong province.

variants.name: Phan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Hoa, Minh, Tuan, Dung, Lan, Son, Long, Vinh, Chau, Huong, Hong, Dong, Sang, Hang, Chi, Chien, Ho, Man, Lai, Chan, Han, Ke, Tam, Nam, Thai, Phong, Tinh, Chung, Thach, Manh, Tuong, Chang, Sinh, Tham.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 潘, see   Pan   1 and 3 below.
Cambodian: written ផាន or ផាន់, of Chinese origin (see 3 below). Compare   Phann  .

variants.name: Poon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Kam, Wai, Kwok, Cheuk, Sin, Chi Ming, Ching, Chung Yin, Ho, Kam Po, Keung, Lai Ping.
Chinese:
Cantonese form of the surnames 潘 and 盤, see   Pan   1 and 2.

variants.name: Pun
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ching, Chi Keung, Hong, Kin, Cheung, Fu, Ho, Jung, Kam, Kam Cheung, Kee, Kin Chung.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 潘 and 盤, see   Pan   1 and 2.

variants.name: Phang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Kok, Chyi, Heng, Hieng, Kin Wai, Kiu, Ming, Peng, Sang, Sarin, Song, Wai, Yan, Coong, Hinh, Tran Van, Tri, Trinh, Chung.

Southeast Asian (Hmong): from the name of the Phang or Pha clan of the Hmong people in Laos, China, and Vietnam; in Chinese characters it is written 潘 (see   Pan   1, compare 4 below). Compare   Pha   2.
Cambodian: written ផង់, probably of Chinese origin (compare below).

variants.name: Pua
meanings: Chinese:
Teochew or Hokkien form of the surname 潘, see   Pan   1.
variant Romanization of the surname 傅, see   Fu   1.

variants.name: Ban
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yoshimi, Akihiko, Hiroki, Junji, Keiko, Keisuke, Koji, Masanori, Masataka, Misa, Misao, Naoki.  Korean Yong, Young, Chea, Chun Hung, Chunsheng, Dong, Eun, Hee, Hyun, Hyung.  Hungarian Zoltan, Bela, Imre, Laszlo, Tibor.

Croatian, Slovenian, and Serbian; Hungarian and Slovak (Bán): nickname from Hungarian bán, South Slavic ban, or Slovak bán ‘governor’. Hungarian Bán was also used as a personal name. Historically, the word ban is found mostly as a Croatian status name for a regional military or administrative leader. The surname possibly denoted a person in the service of a ban or one who came from a land under the authority of a ban. As a surname of Hungarian origin Ban is also common in Romania.
Slovenian: from a short form of the personal name   Urban  .

variants.name: Pon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Chan, Cheu, Cheuk, Chi, Chow, Chu, Guo Hui, Kim Hung, Sin, Sing, Wai, Wing.
English: unexplained.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 潘 and 盤, see   Pan   1 and 2.

nameMeanings.name: Herbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from heri, hari ‘army’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. An Old English form, Herebeorht, existed in England before the Conquest, but was superseded by the Norman form, which gave rise to an important surname. The family in question were earls of Pembroke in the 16th and 17th centuries; they included the poet George Herbert . By the end of the Middle Ages Herbert was little used, although it remained a favourite with some families, notably the Saint Quintins of East Yorkshire. Its greater frequency in Britain from the 19th century onwards is due partly to the trend for the revival of medieval names of Germanic origin and partly to the trend for the transferred use of surnames.

variants.name: Heribert
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from heri, hari ‘army’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. An Old English form, Herebeorht, existed in England before the Conquest, but was superseded by the Norman form, which gave rise to an important surname. The family in question were earls of Pembroke in the 16th and 17th centuries; they included the poet George Herbert . By the end of the Middle Ages Herbert was little used, although it remained a favourite with some families, notably the Saint Quintins of East Yorkshire. Its greater frequency in Britain from the 19th century onwards is due partly to the trend for the revival of medieval names of Germanic origin and partly to the trend for the transferred use of surnames.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

en.lifeSummary: When Herbert de Pyn was born in 1140, in Upton Pyne, Devon, England, his father, Simon de Pyn, was 20 and his mother, Mrs Simon de Pyn, was 19. He had at least 1 son with Mrs Herbert de Pyn. He died in 1219, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 79.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Herbert de Pyn est né en 1140, à Upton Pyne, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Simon de Pyn, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Mrs Simon de Pyn, avait 19 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Mrs Herbert de Pyn. Il est décédé en 1219, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 79 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Herbert de Pyn 1140 in Upton Pyne, Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Simon de Pyn 20 und seine Mutter Mrs Simon de Pyn 19. Er hatte mit Mrs Herbert de Pyn mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1219 im Alter von 79 Jahren in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Herbert de Pyn è nato il nel 1140 nel Upton Pyne, Devon, England, il suo padre, Simon de Pyn, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Mrs Simon de Pyn, aveva 19 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Mrs Herbert de Pyn. Morì nel 1219, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 79 anni.

ja.Upton Pyne, Devon, England: イギリスデボンウプトン プイン
ja.lifeSummary: Herbert de Pynが1140年にイギリスデボンウプトン プインで生まれた時，父Simon de Pynは20歳，母Mrs Simon de Pynは19歳であった。彼はMrs Herbert de Pynとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1219年にイギリスイギリスで79歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Herbert de Pyn(이)가 1140년 영국 데폰 업턴 핀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Simon de Pyn(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs Simon de Pyn(은)는 19세였다. 그는 Mrs Herbert de Pyn(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1219년 영국 잉글랜드에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Herbert de Pyn nasceu em 1140, em Upton Pyne, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Simon de Pyn, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Mrs Simon de Pyn, tinha 19 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Mrs Herbert de Pyn. Ele faleceu em 1219, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 79 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Herbert de Pyn родился в 1140 г. в местности уптон пин, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени Simon de Pyn было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs Simon de Pyn, 19. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Mrs Herbert de Pyn. Он умер в 1219 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 79 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Herbert de Pyn nació en 1140, en Upton Pyne, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Simon de Pyn, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Mrs Simon de Pyn, tenía 19 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Mrs Herbert de Pyn. Murió en 1219, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 79 años.
